↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner 🇨🇳 China, after a nearly decade-long pause, is returningto active land reclamation in the northern part of the South China Sea, transforming one of the reefs into a major military base. According to satellite images, about 6 square kilometers of new land have been formed over several months, elevating the facility to the level of China's largest advanced bases. Satellite images already show docks, an artificial lagoon capable of accommodating large ships, helicopter pads, and a leveled coastline that is likely being prepared for a full-fledged runway for aircraft. In this case, we are talking about the Paracel Islands - the northern part of the water area, where China has de facto control, but where claims from Vietnam and Taiwan still persist. In a broader context, this is a continuation of China's strategy to consolidate control over a key maritime artery, through which a significant portion of global trade passes and which is of critical importance in the scenario around Taiwan. Unlike remote positions on more distant islands, the infrastructure in the Paracel Islands is closer to mainland China, easier to supply, and less vulnerable to blockades, making it more suitable for intensive regular operations. The land reclamation not only allows for the expansion of territory but also the deployment of a full-fledged complex: an airfield, air defense, anti-ship weapons, and the basing of a fleet, including large surface ships. See the latest updates with us: @visionergeo tweet

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇮🇱 Israeli Channel 12: New details have emerged about the rescue of the American F‑15E navigator shot down over Iran. The officer was unconscious with a concussion and did not transmit a distress signal initially. He made first contact at 12:00 on Friday, climbed to the highest point to avoid detection, walked 10‑12 kilometres and hid in a crevice, from where he sent precise coordinates on Friday night. Israel refrained from striking in the area and provided intelligence. During Friday and Saturday, Israel was asked to assist with air superiority and struck relevant targets. U.S. forces seized a farm 18 kilometres from his hideout, landing two aircraft and small helicopters. The helicopters extracted him from the crevice and returned him to the farm. During takeoff, two C‑130s became stuck in sand; three smaller planes were called to evacuate the navigator and special forces. Fighter jets then bombed the two stranded aircraft to prevent capture. @wfwitness

The government will handle…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iranian MP Abdolhossein Rouhalamini stated U.S. President Trump wants to destroy and “swallow” Iran to take its oil, and that the aim is to partition the country. He argued that Iran’s ethnic groups would not agree to division and that such threats are bluffs. Rouhalamini acknowledged that Iran’s air defence was weak, but noted that Tehran’s skies are no longer as they were a month ago. He claimed Iran hit a U.S. AWACS costing tens of millions of dollars, adding that no air defence is 100% effective – not even US systems – and that American soldiers are hiding in hotels out of fear. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 The Israeli Air Force said it struck Hezbollah's alleged command centers in Beirut's southern suburbs, as well as two gas stations owned by the Al-Amana fuel distribution company elsewhere in Lebanon. The military said Hezbollah used the headquarters to allegedly plan attacks on troops and Israeli civilians, and that the fuel stations generated millions of dollars for the group's activities. They added that since the start of the war, the IDF has struck more than 15 Al-Amana stations, which are under US sanctions. @wfwitness

A 25-year-old British flight attendant was arrested in Dubai after sharing a photo of drone strike damage near the airport in a private WhatsApp group. He only asked if it was safe to pass through the airport. Authorities accessed the phone and made the arrest. He faces up to 2 years in prison, large fines, and possible deportation. Source: MEE
